{%- macro check() -%} {%- endmacro -%} {%- macro mockbar(title) -%}
{{ title }}
{%- endmacro -%}

Core building blocks

Six patterns next.dj adds, each shown in real project source.

File router

Pages are files

Add a directory with a page.py under routes/ and it answers a URL. Bracketed segments capture typed parameters.

  • {{ check() }} Directories become URL segments, no urls.py
  • {{ check() }} [int:post_id] captures a typed path parameter
  • {{ check() }} DUrl[int] coerces and injects it by type
{{ mockbar("routes/posts/[int:post_id]/page.py") }} 
from notes.models import Note
from next.pages import context
from next.urls.markers import DUrl


@context("note")
def note(post_id: DUrl[int]) -> Note:
    return Note.objects.get(pk=post_id)
Components

Composable components

Call components by name, pass props, and fill named slots with child markup.

  • {{ check() }} One tag, {% raw %}{% component %}{% endraw %}, for every component
  • {{ check() }} Props are explicit, slots fill block content
  • {{ check() }} Co-locate CSS and JS in the component folder
{{ mockbar("routes/template.djx") }} 
{% raw %}{% #component{% endraw %} "card" title="Welcome" %}
  {% raw %}{% #slot{% endraw %} "content" %}
    <p>Latest update.</p>
  {% raw %}{% /slot %}{% endraw %}
{% raw %}{% /component %}{% endraw %}
Forms

Forms wired to handlers

Register a handler with @action and bind a form class. CSRF and re-render are handled for you.

  • {{ check() }} @action registers a POST endpoint by name
  • {{ check() }} form_class binds and validates the body
  • {{ check() }} Invalid submissions re-render with errors
{{ mockbar("routes/page.py") }} 
from django.http import HttpResponseRedirect
from next.forms import action
from notes.forms import NoteForm


@action("create_note", form_class=NoteForm)
def create_note(form: NoteForm) -> HttpResponseRedirect:
    form.save()
    return HttpResponseRedirect("/")
Static assets

Assets next to pages

Drop a template.css or component.js beside the file that uses it. The pipeline collects each asset and injects it where the page renders.

  • {{ check() }} Asset files pair with a page or component by filename
  • {{ check() }} Component assets ship only when the component renders
  • {{ check() }} {% raw %}{% collect_styles %}{% endraw %} emits the deduplicated output
{{ mockbar("notes/") }} 
routes/
  page.py
  template.djx
  template.css        # collected with the page
  template.js
_components/note_card/
  component.djx
  component.css       # collected with the component
  component.js
Dependency injection

Inject by signature

Declare what a page or action needs as typed parameters. The resolver matches each one to a provider and supplies it.

  • {{ check() }} Parameters resolve by name, type, and marker
  • {{ check() }} @resolver.dependency publishes a named value
  • {{ check() }} Depends("name") pulls it into any signature
{{ mockbar("routes/notes/[int:note_id]/page.py") }} 
from django.http import HttpRequest
from next.deps import Depends
from next.pages import context
from next.urls.markers import DUrl


@context("note")
def note(
    request: HttpRequest,
    note_id: DUrl[int],
    repo: NoteRepo = Depends("note_repo"),
) -> Note:
    return repo.for_user(request.user).get(pk=note_id)
Testing

Tests that know the router

NextClient boots the file router and registers actions, so a test posts to an action by name.

  • {{ check() }} NextClient extends Django's test client
  • {{ check() }} post_action resolves an action to its URL
  • {{ check() }} render_page covers template-only assertions
{{ mockbar("notes/tests/test_actions.py") }} 
from next.testing.client import NextClient


def test_create_note():
    response = NextClient().post_action(
        "create_note", {"title": "Test", "body": ""},
    )
    assert response.status_code == 302
